name: Zambia
avoid: November to April is wetter and greener, but safari roads get slower, Victoria Falls spray can limit viewpoints, and walking safari logistics are less predictable
Proflight Zambia seats to Mfuwe, Jeki, and Livingstone can sell out quickly in the dry season, so lock flights before lodging if you want the smoothest route
Night driving between Lusaka, Livingstone, Siavonga, and the park access roads is best avoided because of potholes, livestock, and limited roadside lighting
stopoverNote: Lusaka is the practical gateway for almost every Zambia itinerary from India. Proflight Zambia is the cleanest way to link Livingstone, Mfuwe, and the Lower Zambezi airstrips, while Siavonga and Lake Kariba fit best as a final road trip from Lusaka.

cities.name: Lusaka
cities.notes: Capital reset for museums, craft shopping, and the key Proflight Zambia connections before the safari sectors begin

cities.name: Livingstone
cities.notes: Base for the Zambian side of Victoria Falls, Zambezi cruises, museum stops, and easy access to Mosi-oa-Tunya

cities.name: South Luangwa National Park
cities.notes: Mfuwe-based safari country known for classic walking safaris, leopard-rich night drives, and some of Zambia's best-value bush camps

cities.name: Lower Zambezi
cities.notes: River-and-escarpment wilderness where canoeing, boat safaris, walking, and tiger fishing define the experience

cities.name: Lake Kariba
cities.notes: Siavonga-side lake escape for dam views, houseboat sunsets, fishing, and a softer final day after bush flights and game drives

itinerary.sim: Airtel Zambia is the safest all-round SIM for this loop, with MTN Zambia also workable in Lusaka and Livingstone. Budget about ₹310–₹1,240 for a starter SIM or eSIM with enough data for maps, WhatsApp, and airline confirmations, but expect patchier service inside South Luangwa and the Lower Zambezi camps.

days.theme: Arrival reset, capital errands, and setting up the first domestic hop or long bus leg

activities.name: Lusaka National Museum entry
Kabwata Cultural Village craft browse
Lilayi Elephant Nursery visit or donation-based stop
Proflight Zambia one-way flight from Lusaka to Livingstone
Mazhandu Family Bus Services or Shalom Bus Services coach from Lusaka to Livingstone

days.theme: Victoria Falls viewpoints, local history, and the classic first sunset on the Zambezi

activities.name: Mosi-oa-Tunya National Park / Victoria Falls entry on the Zambian side
Knife-Edge Bridge and main rainforest viewpoints walk
Livingstone Museum entry
Lady Livingstone sunset cruise on the Zambezi
Mukuni Park Curio Market browse

days.theme: Adrenaline or wildlife add-ons before turning the trip fully toward safari country

activities.name: Batoka Sky helicopter or microlight flight over Victoria Falls
Mukuni Village guided cultural visit
Mosi-oa-Tunya game drive or white rhino walk
Royal Livingstone Express dining excursion
Rental car pickup for a Zambia road segment if you prefer not to fly every leg

hotels.name: The David Livingstone Safari Lodge & Spa

days.theme: Fly into Mfuwe, settle into the Luangwa Valley, and start with the classic sunset safari rhythm

activities.name: Proflight Zambia one-way flight from Lusaka to Mfuwe
South Luangwa National Park entry fee for international visitors
Shared sunset game drive from Mfuwe Lodge, Kafunta River Lodge, or Flatdogs Camp
Leopard-focused night drive with lodge guide and scout
Airstrip or lodge road transfer around Mfuwe

days.theme: Walking safari country, village context, and another strong wildlife day without changing camps

activities.name: Guided walking safari from Flatdogs Camp, Robin Pope Safaris, or Kafunta Safaris
Morning game drive back toward the Luangwa River oxbows
Kawaza Village cultural visit near Mfuwe
Tribal Textiles showroom and workshop stop
Optional second park day fee if your lodge bills each safari date separately

meals: Track and Trail River Camp Restaurant

days.theme: Bush flight arrival, river frontage, and the signature canoe-and-sundowner pairing

activities.name: Proflight Zambia one-way flight from Lusaka to Jeki or Royal airstrip
Lower Zambezi National Park entry fee for international visitors
Shared half-day or full-day canoe safari from Royal Zambezi Lodge, Chiawa Camp, or Chongwe River Camp
Park canoeing activity fee where charged separately
Sunset boat safari on the Zambezi channels

days.theme: Mix a land safari with river time and keep one premium experience for Zambia's most polished wilderness zone

activities.name: Morning game drive with guide and scout in the Lower Zambezi floodplain
Guided walking safari in the lower valley concessions
Tiger fishing trip on the Zambezi
Boat transfer or lodge shuttle between Jeki and your riverside camp
Optional second park day fee if your camp invoices by full calendar day

days.theme: End with Siavonga views, the lakefront, and a lighter Zambia finale after the park-heavy middle stretch

activities.name: Rental car from Lusaka for the Siavonga and Lake Kariba road trip
Intercity bus from Lusaka to Siavonga
Kariba Dam wall and viewpoint stop
Lake Kariba sunset cruise or short houseboat outing from Siavonga
Fishing charter or private boat hire on the lake

connections.method: Proflight Zambia is the fastest link; Mazhandu and Shalom buses are the budget fallback if you can spare most of a day on the road
Usually route back through Lusaka and connect onward to Mfuwe with Proflight Zambia; doing it fully overland takes too long for most short Zambia trips
Bush logistics are smoothest via Mfuwe-Lusaka and then Lusaka-Jeki or Royal on Proflight Zambia, usually on the same or next day depending on schedules
Most travelers fly or boat back toward Lusaka first, then continue by rental car or bus to Siavonga for the lake finish
Return by self-drive rental car or intercity bus from Siavonga depending on how much flexibility you want on the final day

extras: May to October is the sweet spot for this route because wildlife gathers near water in South Luangwa and Lower Zambezi, and road conditions are much more reliable than in the rains.

Victoria Falls is at its fullest around late rainy season and early dry season, so May and June give you maximum spray, while August to October gives you drier viewpoints and stronger safari conditions deeper inland.

South Luangwa walking safaris are one of Zambia's signature experiences, but they depend on lodge guiding teams, wildlife movements, and trail conditions, so leave your schedule flexible rather than overbooking every hour.
Lower Zambezi canoeing sometimes prices the river activity separately from park entry, so confirm whether your camp includes the canoe permit before you compare operators.
If you only splurge once, spend it on a premium camp in South Luangwa or Lower Zambezi rather than on every transit leg; the safari guiding quality is where Zambia really overdelivers.

Lake Kariba works best as a decompression stop from Lusaka or on the way to Zimbabwe, not as a substitute for the main safari parks, so keep it short unless you specifically want fishing or a houseboat.
